nodes.label: AI as expedient shortcut undermining learning
nodes.label_negative: AI as tool for genuine skill development

nodes.label: AI for repetitive simple tasks
nodes.grounding_span: I'd rather think of AI as an absolute last resort

nodes.label: AI as brainstorming and idea generator
nodes.label_negative: AI as replacement for own expertise
nodes.grounding_span: I needed the AI to help fill in some gaps for me

nodes.label: Teacher trust in own professional judgment
nodes.grounding_span: I trust myself better than any AI platform

nodes.label: AI detection and policing of student work
nodes.grounding_span: I find myself spending more time utilizing AI-detecting software

nodes.label: Academic writing quality
nodes.grounding_span: develop their style and voice as writers

nodes.label: Professional integrity and ethical practice
nodes.grounding_span: I would feel like a bit of a hypocrite if I used AI extensively

nodes.label: Creative and effective teaching
nodes.grounding_span: became incredibly engaging and novel with some tinkering

nodes.label: Frustration with AI training difficulty
nodes.grounding_span: Feeding a writing prompt into ChatGPT undermines the very purpose of the course

nodes.label: Resignation to AI inevitability
nodes.grounding_span: a sense of resignation; a feeling that I cannot battle something so profoundly massive

nodes.label: Satisfaction with AI for brainstorming
nodes.grounding_span: Turning to AI in this instance helped supplement the knowledge I already had and really served as a brainstorming tool

nodes.label: Optimism about AI as future tool
nodes.grounding_span: I'm going to be cautiously optimistic about AI in the classroom

nodes.label: Iterative refinement with limited back-and-forth
nodes.grounding_span: I definitely go back and forth. I will ask the AI to modify prose, or audience, or reconsider word count

nodes.label: Experience-driven calibration of trust
nodes.grounding_span: I felt less expert in the domain I was attempting to lesson plan for
